## Overview

The ui-ux-pro-max-skill is an AI skill that provides design intelligence for building professional UI/UX across multiple platforms and frameworks.

## Repository Details

- **Repository URL**: https://github.com/nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill
- **Owner**: nextlevelbuilder
- **License**: MIT License
- It has 36k stars, 3.5k forks, and was last updated on Feb 27, 2026

## Key Features

The flagship feature of v2.0 is the Design System Generator - an AI-powered reasoning engine that analyzes your project requirements and generates a complete, tailored design system in seconds.

The plugin features a knowledge base with 57 UI styles, 95 color palettes, 56 font combinations, and works as a skill/workflow for AI coding assistants.

## Supported Platforms

The tool supports HTML/Tailwind, React, Next.js, Vue, Nuxt.js, Nuxt UI, Svelte, ShadCN, Flutter, SwiftUI, React Native, and Jetpack Compose

## How It Works

Users can request UI/UX tasks, and the AI automatically generates a complete design system, finds the best matching styles, colors, and typography based on product type and requirements, and implements the UI with proper colors, fonts, spacing, and best practices.

```text
# UI UX Pro Max - AI Design Intelligence Skill

## Overview

UI UX Pro Max is an AI skill providing design system intelligence for building professional interfaces across multiple platforms and frameworks. It features 67 UI styles, 96 color palettes, 57 font pairings, and 100 industry-specific reasoning rules.

## Key Features

**Design Assets:**
- "67 UI Styles" ranging from minimalism to cyberpunk aesthetics
- "96 Color Palettes" organized by industry (SaaS, healthcare, fintech, beauty)
- "57 Font Pairings" with Google Fonts integration
- 25 chart type recommendations for dashboards
- 13 supported tech stacks

**Intelligent Generation:**
The v2.0 flagship feature is an automated design system generator using reasoning engines that analyze project requirements and generate complete design systems in seconds.

**Industry Coverage:**
Rules tailored for tech, finance, healthcare, e-commerce, services, creative fields, and emerging tech sectors.

## Installation

### Via CLI (Recommended)

```bash
npm install -g uipro-cli
cd /path/to/your/project
uipro init --ai claude    # Claude Code
uipro init --ai cursor    # Cursor
uipro init --ai windsurf  # Windsurf
uipro init --ai all       # All assistants
```

### Via Claude Marketplace

```
/plugin marketplace add nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill
/plugin install ui-ux-pro-max@ui-ux-pro-max-skill
```

### Prerequisites

Python 3.x is required for the search script functionality.

## Usage Modes

**Skill Mode (Auto-activate):**
Supported in Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and others. Simply request UI/UX work naturally in chat.

**Workflow Mode (Slash Command):**
For Kiro, GitHub Copilot, and Roo Code:
```
/ui-ux-pro-max Build a landing page for my SaaS product
```

## Supported Tech Stacks

Web frameworks include React, Next.js, Vue, Nuxt.js, Svelte, and Astro. Mobile: SwiftUI, Jetpack Compose, React Native, Flutter. Default: HTML + Tailwind.

## Advanced Features

**Design System Generation:**
Command-line access to reasoning engine for domain-specific searches and design system persistence using a Master + Overrides pattern for hierarchical retrieval across sessions.

**Persistence Structure:**
```
design-system/
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ MASTER.md           # Global source of truth
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ pages/
    ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ [page-name].md  # Page-specific overrides
```

## Support & Resources

- Repository: github.com/nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill
- Website: uupm.cc
- 35.9k GitHub stars, 3.5k forks
- Includes 99 UX guidelines and anti-pattern documentation
```

# CLAUDE.md Content Summary

## Overview
This file guides Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with the "Antigravity Kit" ÔÇô an AI-powered design intelligence toolkit. It provides searchable databases of UI styles, color palettes, font pairings, chart types, and UX guidelines functioning as a skill/workflow for AI coding assistants.

## Search Command Syntax
```
python3 src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<query>" --domain <domain> [-n <max_results>]
```

**Available domains:** product, style, typography, color, landing, chart, ux

**Stack-specific search:**
```
python3 src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<query>" --stack <stack>
```

Supported stacks include html-tailwind (default), react, nextjs, astro, vue, nuxtjs, nuxt-ui, svelte, swiftui, react-native, flutter, shadcn, and jetpack-compose.

## Architecture
The source of truth resides in `src/ui-ux-pro-max/`. The system uses symlinks for `.claude/`, `.factory/`, and `.shared/` directories. The search engine combines BM25 ranking with regex matching, supporting automatic domain detection when `--domain` is omitted.

## Sync Rules
- **Edit data & scripts** in `src/ui-ux-pro-max/`; changes propagate automatically via symlinks
- **Update templates** in `src/ui-ux-pro-max/templates/` (base/ and platforms/)
- **Before publishing CLI**, copy assets: `cp -r src/ui-ux-pro-max/{data,scripts,templates}/* cli/assets/`

## Prerequisites
Requires Python 3.x with no external dependencies.

## Git Workflow
Never push directly to `main`. Instead: create a branch (`feat/...` or `fix/...`), commit, push, then submit a PR via `gh pr create`.
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
# UI/UX Pro Max Skill Overview

## Core Purpose
This skill provides comprehensive design intelligence for web and mobile applications, offering "50+ styles, 97 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 99 UX guidelines, and 25 chart types across 9 technology stacks."

## How It Operates

**Primary Workflow:**
1. Analyze user requirements (product type, style, industry, tech stack)
2. Generate design system using `--design-system` flag (REQUIRED step)
3. Supplement with domain-specific searches as needed
4. Apply stack-specific guidelines
5. Implement with pre-delivery checklist

**Key Command Structure:**
```bash
python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<query>" --design-system [-p "Project Name"]
```

## Eight Priority Categories

The skill organizes guidance by impact level:

| Priority | Category | Impact |
|----------|----------|--------|
| 1-2 | Accessibility, Touch/Interaction | CRITICAL |
| 3-4 | Performance, Layout/Responsive | HIGH |
| 5-6 | Typography, Animation | MEDIUM |
| 7-8 | Style Selection, Charts | MEDIUM-LOW |

## Searchable Domains

Users can target specific knowledge areas: `product`, `style`, `typography`, `color`, `landing`, `chart`, `ux`, `react`, `web`, and `prompt`.

## Activation Triggers

Use when designing UI components, choosing colors/fonts, reviewing code for UX issues, building dashboards/landing pages, or implementing accessibility requirements.

## Notable Feature: Persistent Design Systems

The `--persist` flag creates hierarchical documentation: a master design system with optional page-specific overrides, enabling consistent multi-page projects.
```
</details></tool-use>

     3Ôćĺ## 1. Color System
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺThe color palette utilizes fixed brand inputs structured explicitly into Light Mode (Sonata) and Dark Mode (Nocturne). Semantic meaning is strictly bound to color roles.

    22Ôćĺ| **Detail** | Deep Cocoa | `#2D1817` | Borders, secondary text, inactive states, staff lines. | `theme.nocturne.detail` |
    23Ôćĺ| **Accent** | Amber Glow | `#FFB300` | Focus states, active selection, interactive handles. | `theme.nocturne.accent` |
    24Ôćĺ

    26Ôćĺ

    35Ôćĺ

    46Ôćĺ## 2. Typography System
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ**Typefaces**
    49Ôćĺ

    51Ôćĺ* **Body (Content):** Inter

    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ---
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ## 3. Spacing, Radius & Geometry
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ**Spacing System (8-Point Grid)**
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ* `2px` (Minor) ÔÇö Gap between grouped monospace data items.
    75Ôćĺ* `4px` (Tight) ÔÇö Padding inside small interactive toggles or input boundaries.
    76Ôćĺ* `8px` (Base) ÔÇö Margins parsing list items within the Theory Inspector.
    77Ôćĺ* `16px` (Default) ÔÇö Standard padding for Surface Cards and Modal dialogues.
    78Ôćĺ* `32px` (Macro) ÔÇö Spatial separation between Sandbox and Sidebar.
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ**Border Radius System**
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ* `0px` (Sharp) ÔÇö Edges connected to the VexFlow canvas, red logic lines, and algorithmic constraints.
    83Ôćĺ* `2px` (Micro) ÔÇö Tooltips and metadata pills.
    84Ôćĺ* `4px` (Standard) ÔÇö Interactive buttons, overrides, and toggles (signifying human touch).
    85Ôćĺ* `50%` (Circular) ÔÇö Singularly reserved for the draggable noteheads.
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ---
    88Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺ## 4. Layout & Responsiveness
    90Ôćĺ
    91ÔćĺTo ensure the Glass-box UI degrades gracefully while preserving the VexFlow canvas:
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ* **Breakpoint Constraints:**
    94Ôćĺ  * `lg` (Ôëą1024px): 70% Sandbox / 30% Theory Inspector (Side-by-Side).
    95Ôćĺ  * `md` (768px - 1023px): 60% Sandbox / 40% Theory Inspector.
    96Ôćĺ  * `sm` (<768px): Stacked. The `InteractiveTactileSandbox` takes 100% width. The `TheoryInspectorSidebar` becomes a bottom-anchored pull-up drawer (BottomSheet) triggered by selection.
    97Ôćĺ* **VexFlow Scaling:** The VexFlow SVG target `div` must NOT use fixed pixel widths. It must use `w-full h-full` and rely on a `ResizeObserver` hook to trigger a re-render of `Vex.Flow.Factory` when container dimensions change.
    98Ôćĺ* **Typography Scaling:** Use CSS `clamp()` for headings to prevent horizontal overflow in Inspector panels.
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ---
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ## 5. Motion & Interaction Rules
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ* **Note Drag Behavior:** Notes move discretely via a snapping coordinate system corresponding to semitones/diatonic steps. Continuous, fluid dragging (analog-style physical interpolation) is forbidden to enforce strict symbolic logic.
   105Ôćĺ* **Violation Reveal Timing:** Zero latency. Red Line systems appear simultaneously with the drop event/keyboard confirmation. No "fade-in" animations are permitted.
   106Ôćĺ* **Repair Transitions:** When switching between user state and recommended repair state, employ a crisp `0ms` swap. Linear interpolation (`100ms max`) reserved strictly for programmatic voice-leading paths in SVG nodes only.
   107Ôćĺ* **Forbidden Animation:** No bouncing, spring physics, or easing functions that imply weight or physical realism.
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ---
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ## 6. Iconography System
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ* **Style:** Sharp, linear, 1.5px stroke weight (2px on hover). Filled icons are explicitly forbidden unless representing active global state booleans.
   114Ôćĺ* **Visual Metaphors:** Avoid consumer UI metaphors (no "magic wands" for generation). Use drafting and mechanical metaphors (e.g., compasses, set squares, logic gates).
   115Ôćĺ* **Semantic Geometry:**
   116Ôćĺ  * **Sharp Angles (Triangles/Rectangles):** Denote algorithmic rules, calculations, and constraints.
   117Ôćĺ  * **Curves/Circles:** Denote user input, human interaction, or override functions.
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ---
   120Ôćĺ
   121Ôćĺ## 7. Accessibility, Operability, Content & Voice (POUR)
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ**1. Perceivable & Operable**
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ* **Color Independence:** Semantic color cues must always be paired with secondary visual signifiers (e.g., solid line vs. dashed line).
   126Ôćĺ* **Edit-Authority via Keyboard:** Full edit-authority of the VexFlow state must be possible via Arrow and Modifier keys without a pointer device. Tab sequences must fluidly navigate between the Sandbox and Inspector layers.
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ**2. Understandable & Robust**
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ* **Screen Reader Handling:** Draggable Note Tokens inject ARIA labels formatted as `[Voice]: [Pitch] [Duration]` (e.g., `Soprano: C5 Quarter Note`).
   131Ôćĺ* **Live Regions for Causality:** Rule violations announce immediately as aria-live critical alerts: `Alert: Parallel Fifth detected between Soprano and Tenor`.
   132Ôćĺ* **Semantic DOM Elements:** SVG interaction must map to standard, accessible HTML `<button>` and `<input>` proxy elements within the React application tree.
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ**3. Content & Voice Guidelines**
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ* **Tone:** Scholarly, objective, calm, and non-judgmental. Do not use conversational filler or emotional validation.
   137Ôćĺ* **AI Explanations:** Confined strictly to the Theory Inspector panel to prevent cluttering the Sandbox. Use precise language like "Stylistic Observation: Doubled Leading Tone."
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ---
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ## 8. Developer Hand-off & Schema Definition
   142Ôćĺ
   143ÔćĺTo effectively generate production-ready React/VexFlow code without hallucination, all components and logic must adhere to the following contracts.
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ### A. State Management & Data Ontology (Zustand)
   146Ôćĺ
   147ÔćĺThe core of the application must rely on a strictly typed, immutable representation of musical state.
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ```typescript
   150Ôćĺ// types/music.ts
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ/**
   153Ôćĺ * Employs the "Theory Named" strategy. 
   154Ôćĺ * Pitches must be explicitly defined using scientific pitch notation.
   155Ôćĺ */
   156Ôćĺexport type PitchClass = 'C' | 'C#' | 'D' | 'D#' | 'E' | 'F' | 'F#' | 'G' | 'G#' | 'A' | 'A#' | 'B';
   157Ôćĺexport type Octave = 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 | 7;
   158Ôćĺexport type Duration = 'w' | 'h' | 'q' | '8' | '16'; // VexFlow compatible
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺexport interface NoteEvent {
   161Ôćĺ  id: string; // Unique UUID
   162Ôćĺ  pitch: `${PitchClass}${Octave}`; 
   163Ôćĺ  duration: Duration;
   164Ôćĺ  voice: 'Soprano' | 'Alto' | 'Tenor' | 'Bass';
   165Ôćĺ  isUserModified: boolean; // Triggers UI 'Accent' color states
   166Ôćĺ}
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺexport interface TheoryViolation {
   169Ôćĺ  id: string;
   170Ôćĺ  ruleId: string; // e.g., 'RULE_PARALLEL_FIFTH'
   171Ôćĺ  severity: 'Violation' | 'Warning';
   172Ôćĺ  involvedNoteIds: string[]; // Maps directly to Red Line SVGs
   173Ôćĺ  isOverridden: boolean;
   174Ôćĺ}
   175Ôćĺ
   176Ôćĺ// store/useScoreStore.ts
   177Ôćĺexport interface ScoreState {
   178Ôćĺ  notes: NoteEvent[];
   179Ôćĺ  violations: TheoryViolation[];
   180Ôćĺ  selectedNoteId: string | null;
   181Ôćĺ  moveNote: (id: string, newPitch: `${PitchClass}${Octave}`) => void;
   182Ôćĺ  toggleOverride: (violationId: string) => void;
   183Ôćĺ}
   184Ôćĺ```
   185Ôćĺ
   186Ôćĺ### B. VexFlow / React Boundary Constraints
   187Ôćĺ
   188ÔćĺDirect manipulation of the VexFlow canvas via virtual DOM updates is forbidden.
   189Ôćĺ
   190Ôćĺ* **The Rule of the Wrapper:** VexFlow rendering logic must be isolated within a `useEffect` hook that triggers re-renders *only* when the symbolic `ScoreState` changes or container resizes.
   191Ôćĺ* **Proxy Elements for POUR:** Invisible HTML `<button>` elements must be positioned absolutely over the VexFlow note coordinates to handle `onFocus`, `onKeyDown`, and `onClick` synthetic events, dispatching updates to Zustand.
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ```typescript
   194Ôćĺ// components/organisms/StaffRenderer.tsx
   195Ôćĺ// Required implementation pattern
   196Ôćĺ
   197Ôćĺexport const StaffRenderer: React.FC = () => {
   198Ôćĺ  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
   199Ôćĺ  const notes = useScoreStore((state) => state.notes);
   200Ôćĺ
   201Ôćĺ  useEffect(() => {
   202Ôćĺ    if (!containerRef.current) return;
   203Ôćĺ    
   204Ôćĺ    // 1. Clear previous canvas
   205Ôćĺ    containerRef.current.innerHTML = '';
   206Ôćĺ    
   207Ôćĺ    // 2. Initialize VexFlow Formatter
   208Ôćĺ    const vf = new Vex.Flow.Factory({
   209Ôćĺ      renderer: { elementId: containerRef.current, width: containerRef.current.clientWidth, height: 200 }
   210Ôćĺ    });
   211Ôćĺ    
   212Ôćĺ    // 3. Map strictly typed NoteEvents to Vex.Flow.StaveNote
   213Ôćĺ    // ... Imperative VexFlow logic here ...
   214Ôćĺ    
   215Ôćĺ    vf.draw();
   216Ôćĺ  }, [notes]); // Re-render strictly bounded to state determinism AND resize events
   217Ôćĺ
   218Ôćĺ  return <div ref={containerRef} className="relative w-full h-full" />;
   219Ôćĺ};
   220Ôćĺ```
   221Ôćĺ
   222Ôćĺ### C. React Component Library (Atomic Specification)
   223Ôćĺ
   224ÔćĺLLMs must implement the following Prop interfaces exactly as defined. Do not generate arbitrary local state; all core logic resides in Zustand.
   225Ôćĺ
   226Ôćĺ**Atoms**
   227Ôćĺ
   228Ôćĺ```typescript
   229Ôćĺinterface SemanticLabelProps {
   230Ôćĺ  type: 'Violation' | 'Warning' | 'Override' | 'Valid';
   231Ôćĺ  text: string;
   232Ôćĺ  className?: string;
   233Ôćĺ}
   234Ôćĺ
   235Ôćĺinterface ToggleSwitchBaseProps {
   236Ôćĺ  checked: boolean;
   237Ôćĺ  onChange: (checked: boolean) => void;
   238Ôćĺ  ariaLabel: string;
   239Ôćĺ  disabled?: boolean;
   240Ôćĺ}
   241Ôćĺ```
   242Ôćĺ
   243Ôćĺ**Molecules**
   244Ôćĺ
   245Ôćĺ```typescript
   246Ôćĺinterface DraggableNoteTokenProps {
   247Ôćĺ  id: string; // References NoteEvent UUID
   248Ôćĺ  pitch: string;
   249Ôćĺ  isFocused: boolean;
   250Ôćĺ  hasError: boolean;
   251Ôćĺ  onMove: (newPitch: string) => void;
   252Ôćĺ}
   253Ôćĺ
   254Ôćĺinterface TheoryViolationIndicatorProps {
   255Ôćĺ  id: string;
   256Ôćĺ  severity: 'Violation' | 'Warning';
   257Ôćĺ  points: { x: number; y: number }[]; // Computed BBox from VexFlow
   258Ôćĺ  isOverridden: boolean;
   259Ôćĺ}
   260Ôćĺ
   261Ôćĺinterface ConstraintControlProps {
   262Ôćĺ  currentLevel: 'Strict' | 'Standard' | 'Loose';
   263Ôćĺ  onLevelChange: (level: 'Strict' | 'Standard' | 'Loose') => void;
   264Ôćĺ}
   265Ôćĺ```
   266Ôćĺ
   267Ôćĺ**Organisms**
   268Ôćĺ
   269Ôćĺ```typescript
   270Ôćĺinterface TheoryExplanationCardProps {
   271Ôćĺ  violationId: string;
   272Ôćĺ  ruleId: string;
   273Ôćĺ  severity: 'Violation' | 'Warning';
   274Ôćĺ  academicDescription: string; // Scholarly tone enforced
   275Ôćĺ  isOverridden: boolean;
   276Ôćĺ  onOverrideToggle: (violationId: string) => void;
   277Ôćĺ}
   278Ôćĺ```
   279Ôćĺ
   280Ôćĺ---
   281Ôćĺ
   282Ôćĺ## 9. Music Theory Rendering Axioms
   283Ôćĺ
   284ÔćĺTo utilize the **"Theory Named" strategy** effectively, the UI visually translates axiomatic music logic under these constraints:
   285Ôćĺ
   286Ôćĺ1. **Pitch vs. Spelling Strictness:** The system must differentiate between enharmonic equivalents (e.g., C# vs. Db). Interpreting intervals based on MIDI note numbers is forbidden. VexFlow must receive the exact theoretical spelling from the Backend.
   287Ôćĺ2. **Violation Coordinates (The "Red Line" Tooltips):** When drawing the `TheoryViolationIndicator` molecule over VexFlow output, the bounding box of the specific `StaveNote` instances involved must be calculated. The Red Line is an SVG overlay using React portal logic relative to the `containerRef`, ensuring decoupling from VexFlow computation.
   288Ôćĺ3. **Non-Blocking Edits:** The handler for a `moveNote` action must inherently accept *any* pitch modification. The UI renders the new pitch instantly, and evaluating constraints follows afterwards. The UI must never reject a move action defensively (Expressive Sovereignty).
   289Ôćĺ
   290Ôćĺ---
   291Ôćĺ
   292Ôćĺ## 10. Design Tokens (JSON Base)
   293Ôćĺ
   294ÔćĺThe system leverages hierarchical token mapping via JSON, easily compiled to Tailwind CSS theme schemas.
   295Ôćĺ
   296Ôćĺ```json
   297Ôćĺ{

```text
## UI Pro Max Search Results
**Domain:** ux | **Query:** accessibility keyboard navigation canvas editor
**Source:** ux-guidelines.csv | **Found:** 5 results

### Result 1
- **Category:** Accessibility
- **Issue:** Keyboard Navigation
- **Platform:** Web
- **Description:** All functionality accessible via keyboard
- **Do:** Tab order matches visual order
- **Don't:** Keyboard traps or illogical tab order
- **Code Example Good:** tabIndex for custom order
- **Code Example Bad:** Unreachable elements
- **Severity:** High

### Result 2
- **Category:** Accessibility
- **Issue:** Skip Links
- **Platform:** Web
- **Description:** Allow keyboard users to skip navigation
- **Do:** Provide skip to main content link
- **Don't:** No skip link on nav-heavy pages
- **Code Example Good:** Skip to main content link
- **Code Example Bad:** 100 tabs to reach content
- **Severity:** Medium

### Result 3
- **Category:** Accessibility
- **Issue:** Heading Hierarchy
- **Platform:** Web
- **Description:** Screen readers use headings for navigation
- **Do:** Use sequential heading levels h1-h6
- **Don't:** Skip heading levels or misuse for styling
- **Code Example Good:** h1 then h2 then h3
- **Code Example Bad:** h1 then h4
- **Severity:** Medium

### Result 4
- **Category:** Navigation
- **Issue:** Sticky Navigation
- **Platform:** Web
- **Description:** Fixed nav should not obscure content
- **Do:** Add padding-top to body equal to nav height
- **Don't:** Let nav overlap first section content
- **Code Example Good:** pt-20 (if nav is h-20)
- **Code Example Bad:** No padding compensation
- **Severity:** Medium

### Result 5
- **Category:** Interaction
- **Issue:** Focus States
- **Platform:** All
- **Description:** Keyboard users need visible focus indicators
- **Do:** Use visible focus rings on interactive elements
- **Don't:** Remove focus outline without replacement
- **Code Example Good:** focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500
- **Code Example Bad:** outline-none without alternative
- **Severity:** High
```
</details></tool-use>

- **Don't:** No confirmation of completed action
- **Code Example Good:** Toast notification or checkmark
- **Code Example Bad:** Action completes silently

- **Platform:** All

- **Do:** Add pressed/active state visual change
- **Don't:** No feedback during interaction

## UI Pro Max Search Results
**Domain:** ux | **Query:** color contrast wcag accessibility
**Source:** ux-guidelines.csv | **Found:** 5 results

### Result 1
- **Category:** Accessibility
- **Issue:** Color Contrast
- **Platform:** All
- **Description:** Text must be readable against background
- **Do:** Minimum 4.5:1 ratio for normal text
- **Don't:** Low contrast text
- **Code Example Good:** #333 on white (7:1)
- **Code Example Bad:** #999 on white (2.8:1)
- **Severity:** High

### Result 2
- **Category:** Accessibility
- **Issue:** Color Only
- **Platform:** All
- **Description:** Don't convey information by color alone
- **Do:** Use icons/text in addition to color
- **Don't:** Red/green only for error/success
- **Code Example Good:** Red text + error icon
- **Code Example Bad:** Red border only for error
- **Severity:** High

### Result 3
- **Category:** Typography
- **Issue:** Contrast Readability
- **Platform:** All
- **Description:** Body text needs good contrast
- **Do:** Use darker text on light backgrounds
- **Don't:** Gray text on gray background
- **Code Example Good:** text-gray-900 on white
- **Code Example Bad:** text-gray-400 on gray-100
- **Severity:** High

### Result 4
- **Category:** Accessibility
- **Issue:** Alt Text
- **Platform:** All
- **Description:** Images need text alternatives
- **Do:** Descriptive alt text for meaningful images
- **Don't:** Empty or missing alt attributes
- **Code Example Good:** alt='Dog playing in park'
- **Code Example Bad:** alt='' for content images
- **Severity:** High

### Result 5
- **Category:** Accessibility
- **Issue:** Error Messages
- **Platform:** All
- **Description:** Error messages must be announced
- **Do:** Use aria-live or role=alert for errors
- **Don't:** Visual-only error indication
- **Code Example Good:** role='alert'
- **Code Example Bad:** Red border only
- **Severity:** High
```
</details></tool-use>

     5Ôćĺ# UI/UX Pro Max - Design Intelligence
     6Ôćĺ
     7ÔćĺComprehensive design guide for web and mobile applications. Contains 67 styles, 96 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 99 UX guidelines, and 25 chart types across 13 technology stacks. Searchable database with priority-based recommendations.
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ## When to Apply
    10Ôćĺ
    11ÔćĺReference these guidelines when:
    12Ôćĺ- Designing new UI components or pages
    13Ôćĺ- Choosing color palettes and typography
    14Ôćĺ- Reviewing code for UX issues
    15Ôćĺ- Building landing pages or dashboards
    16Ôćĺ- Implementing accessibility requirements
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ## Rule Categories by Priority
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ| Priority | Category | Impact | Domain |
    21Ôćĺ|----------|----------|--------|--------|
    22Ôćĺ| 1 | Accessibility | CRITICAL | `ux` |
    23Ôćĺ| 2 | Touch & Interaction | CRITICAL | `ux` |
    24Ôćĺ| 3 | Performance | HIGH | `ux` |
    25Ôćĺ| 4 | Layout & Responsive | HIGH | `ux` |
    26Ôćĺ| 5 | Typography & Color | MEDIUM | `typography`, `color` |
    27Ôćĺ| 6 | Animation | MEDIUM | `ux` |
    28Ôćĺ| 7 | Style Selection | MEDIUM | `style`, `product` |
    29Ôćĺ| 8 | Charts & Data | LOW | `chart` |
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ## Quick Reference
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ### 1. Accessibility (CRITICAL)
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ- `color-contrast` - Minimum 4.5:1 ratio for normal text
    36Ôćĺ- `focus-states` - Visible focus rings on interactive elements
    37Ôćĺ- `alt-text` - Descriptive alt text for meaningful images
    38Ôćĺ- `aria-labels` - aria-label for icon-only buttons
    39Ôćĺ- `keyboard-nav` - Tab order matches visual order
    40Ôćĺ- `form-labels` - Use label with for attribute
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ### 2. Touch & Interaction (CRITICAL)
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ- `touch-target-size` - Minimum 44x44px touch targets
    45Ôćĺ- `hover-vs-tap` - Use click/tap for primary interactions
    46Ôćĺ- `loading-buttons` - Disable button during async operations
    47Ôćĺ- `error-feedback` - Clear error messages near problem
    48Ôćĺ- `cursor-pointer` - Add cursor-pointer to clickable elements
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ### 3. Performance (HIGH)
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ- `image-optimization` - Use WebP, srcset, lazy loading
    53Ôćĺ- `reduced-motion` - Check prefers-reduced-motion
    54Ôćĺ- `content-jumping` - Reserve space for async content
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### 4. Layout & Responsive (HIGH)

# TypeScript & Code Style Patterns

## Stack
- **Framework:** Next.js App Router (TypeScript)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS utility classes only ÔÇö no ad-hoc color values
- **Score Rendering:** VexFlow (symbolic only)
- **Playback:** Tone.js (symbolic event scheduling ÔÇö no raw audio synthesis)
- **State:** Zustand stores
- **LLM Sidebar:** Theory Inspector (REST/streaming API consumer only)

## Code Style Rules
- All components: modular, component-driven, strictly typed TypeScript
- No `any` types ÔÇö define explicit interfaces/types for all props and state
- Zustand stores: typed slices, no implicit `any` in actions
- VexFlow: wrap in typed React components; never manipulate DOM outside `useEffect`
- Tone.js: schedule symbolic events only; no `AudioContext` or raw buffer manipulation
- POUR accessibility: `aria-label`, keyboard nav, and screen-reader annotations required on all interactive elements

## Design Token Rule
Use only HarmonyForge design system tokens:
- Dark palette: **Nocturne**
- Light palette: **Sonata**
- Never introduce ad-hoc hex values or Tailwind color overrides outside the token system

### ui-ux-pro-max Skill ÔÇö Suggestion Protocol
When the ui-ux-pro-max skill surfaces color palette, typography, or style recommendations:
- **Surface them as labelled suggestions** ÔÇö prefix with `[ui-ux-pro-max suggestion]` so the user can evaluate them explicitly.
- **Never auto-apply** color or font values from the skill. Nocturne/Sonata tokens and the HarmonyForge type scale are the locked baseline.
- **UX pattern suggestions** (`--domain ux`, `--domain react`, `--domain web`) may be adopted directly ÔÇö they carry no token risk.
- **Deviations from the locked design system** require Superman's explicit approval before implementation. Present the deviation, its rationale, and the specific token it would override.

## Architecture Boundary
| Concern | Location |
|---|---|
| Score rendering | VexFlow React component |
| Playback scheduling | Tone.js hook (`usePlayback`) |
| Editor state | Zustand store |
| Violation display | `RedLineTooltip` on VexFlow canvas |
| Theory explanation | Theory Inspector sidebar (API consumer) |
| Constraint logic | Backend only ÔÇö never frontend |


This may or may not be related to the current task.</ide_selection>

python3 << 'EOF'
import math

def hex_to_rgb(h):
    h = h.lstrip('#')
    return tuple(int(h[i:i+2], 16) for i in (0, 2, 4))

def linearize(c):
    c = c / 255
    return c / 12.92 if c <= 0.04045 else ((c + 0.055) / 1.055) ** 2.4

def luminance(hex_color):
    r, g, b = hex_to_rgb(hex_color)
    return 0.2126 * linearize(r) + 0.7152 * linearize(g) + 0.0722 * linearize(b)

def contrast(hex1, hex2):
    l1, l2 = luminance(hex1), luminance(hex2)
    lighter, darker = max(l1, l2), min(l1, l2)
    return (lighter + 0.05) / (darker + 0.05)

def grade(ratio, large=False):
    if large:
        aaa = ratio >= 4.5
        aa  = ratio >= 3.0
    else:
        aaa = ratio >= 7.0
        aa  = ratio >= 4.5
    if aaa: return "AAA Ôťů"
    if aa:  return "AA  Ôťů"
    return "FAIL ÔŁî"

```bash
python3 << 'EOF'
# WCAG 2.1 ┬ž1.4.3 ÔÇö Relative Luminance & Contrast Ratio
# Formula: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/#dfn-relative-luminance

def hex_to_rgb(h):
    h = h.lstrip('#')
    return tuple(int(h[i:i+2], 16) / 255.0 for i in (0, 2, 4))

def luminance(hex_color):
    r, g, b = hex_to_rgb(hex_color)
    def chan(c):
        return c / 12.92 if c <= 0.04045 else ((c + 0.055) / 1.055) ** 2.4
    return 0.2126 * chan(r) + 0.7152 * chan(g) + 0.0722 * chan(b)

def contrast(c1, c2):
    l1, l2 = luminance(c1), luminance(c2)
    if l1 < l2: l1, l2 = l2, l1
    return (l1 + 0.05) / (l2 + 0.05)

def grade(ratio, large=False):
    threshold_aa  = 3.0 if large else 4.5
    threshold_aaa = 4.5 if large else 7.0
    if ratio >= threshold_aaa: return "AAA Ôťů"
    if ratio >= threshold_aa:  return "AA  Ôťů"
    return "FAIL ÔŁî"

# ÔöÇÔöÇ Tokens from globals.css ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ

    'text-sub':     '#6b5740',
}

        aaa   = "Ôťů" if r >= 7.0 else ("~" if r >= 4.5 else "ÔŁî")
        aa    = "Ôťů" if r >= 4.5 else "ÔŁî"
        laa   = "Ôťů" if r >= 3.0 else "ÔŁî"
        print(f"  {label:<32} {r:>6.2f}   {'Ôťů' if r>=4.5 else 'ÔŁî':>9}  {'Ôťů' if r>=3.0 else 'ÔŁî':>9}  {aaa:>5}")
EOF

```

## Analysis ÔÇö What This Actually Means

**No action needed on the failures below** ÔÇö they are by architectural design and are not WCAG violations in context:

| "Failing" pair | Why it's safe |
|---|---|
| `detail / bg` (both themes) | Detail is used for **borders and staff lines** ÔÇö a graphic element, not text. WCAG ┬ž1.4.11 Non-text contrast: 3:1 required. This fails even that. **This is the only genuine concern.** |
| `accent / bg` (Sonata) | Accent (`#ffb300` on `#fdf5e6`) is used for **focus rings and handles**, not text. Non-text contrast 3:1 applies ÔÇö at 1.66:1 this is a **real gap.** |
| `semantic / surface` pairings | Violation/Warning/Override indicators are **never placed directly on `surface`** per the design-system forbidden combos. Architecturally safe. |
| `text-primary / surface` (Sonata) | At 2.93 ÔÇö body text on Cherry Wood surface will fail. **This is a real risk** if any text labels are ever placed on surface cards in Sonata. |

---

## Two Genuine Flags

**Flag 1 ÔÇö `detail` as a graphic element (both themes)**
- Sonata `#d2b48c` on `#fdf5e6`: **1.82:1** ÔÇö fails WCAG ┬ž1.4.11 (non-text 3:1)
- Nocturne `#2d1817` on `#3e2723`: **1.21:1** ÔÇö fails WCAG ┬ž1.4.11
- Staff lines are thin SVG paths ÔÇö the spec allows context (they're decorative to sighted users and are screen-reader-invisible) but this is worth flagging for the accessibility audit record.

**Flag 2 ÔÇö `accent` on Sonata bg (focus rings)**
- `#ffb300` on `#fdf5e6`: **1.66:1** ÔÇö fails ┬ž1.4.11 non-text 3:1
- Focus rings are safety-critical for keyboard-only users. This needs your attention before shipping.

---

## Phase 2 Build Recommendation ÔÇö Ordered

Given the audit and the PLAN.md, here's the recommended execution order:

| Step | Task | Why first |
|---|---|---|
| **2a** | `src/app/layout.tsx` ÔÇö root shell, skip-link, theme toggle wiring, font class injection | Everything depends on this. Can't build any component without it. |
| **2b** | `src/app/page.tsx` ÔÇö arrangement screen skeleton (70/30 split at `lg`, bottom dock placeholder) | Establishes the two primary surfaces (Sandbox + Inspector) before any content goes in. |
| **2c** | Theme toggle component (top-right, Zustand-driven) | Low surface area, high visual payoff ÔÇö immediately confirms SonataÔćöNocturne switching works against the tokens. |
| **2d** | Bottom floating dock shell (Apple-style, `fixed bottom` positioning) | Structural ÔÇö Phase 3 instruments/progress slot into it. |
| **3a** | `StaffRenderer` (VexFlow wrapper + `ResizeObserver`) | Core product surface. `useEffect` boundary, proxy `<button>` overlays for POUR. |

---

**One recommendation before 2a:** The `accent / bg` focus ring gap (Flag 2, 1.66:1 in Sonata) should be documented as a known deviation before the scaffold goes up, so the POUR keyboard-nav implementation can account for it intentionally (e.g. adding a `2px solid` border companion to the focus ring to satisfy non-text contrast). This is a design decision that requires your call, Superman ÔÇö I won't touch a token until you approve.

## What You Can Do Now

### 1. Invoke the skill naturally in chat
The skill auto-activates when you ask for UI work. Just describe what you want and it will run `search.py` in the background against its knowledge base before generating output:

> *"Build the Phase 2 arrangement screen layout"*
> *"Review my StaffRenderer component for UX issues"*
> *"What's the right pattern for a floating bottom dock in Next.js?"*

### 2. Run targeted searches manually
The script is at `.claude/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py`. Domains available:

| Domain flag | What it queries | Token risk |
|---|---|---|
| `--domain ux` | 99 accessibility + interaction guidelines | None ÔÇö safe to adopt |
| `--domain react` | 44 Next.js/React performance patterns | None ÔÇö safe to adopt |
| `--domain web` | DOM perf, layout, scroll patterns | None ÔÇö safe to adopt |
| `--domain style` | 67 UI style recommendations | ÔÜá´ŞĆ Surfaced as suggestion only |
| `--domain color` | 96 palettes | ÔÜá´ŞĆ Surfaced as suggestion only |
| `--domain typography` | 57 font pairings | ÔÜá´ŞĆ Surfaced as suggestion only |
| `--domain product` | Product-type pattern matching | ÔÜá´ŞĆ Surfaced as suggestion only |
| `--domain chart` | 25 chart type recommendations | Context-dependent |

### 3. Phase 2 execution ÔÇö where the skill adds real value

Now that the scaffold doesn't exist yet, every Phase 2 component build will automatically benefit from the skill:

- **Layout patterns** ÔÇö it knows the right split-pane, dock, and drawer patterns for Next.js/Tailwind before you write a line
- **POUR compliance checks** ÔÇö after you write a component, ask it to review for accessibility issues (`--domain ux`)
- **React performance** ÔÇö `StaffRenderer` re-render optimization, `ResizeObserver` patterns, `useEffect` boundaries (`--domain react`)

### 4. Style deviations ÔÇö opt-in process
If you ever want to explore a style direction outside Nocturne/Sonata (e.g. for the landing page noise mesh gradient in Phase 4), the workflow is:

1. Ask for a style suggestion Ôćĺ I run `--domain style` or `--domain color`
2. Result surfaces as `[ui-ux-pro-max suggestion]` with the specific token it would override
3. You approve or reject ÔÇö nothing changes until you say so

### 5. What it won't do / watch out for
- It doesn't know HarmonyForge's domain (music notation, SATB, VexFlow). Domain-specific design decisions still come from the design system + your judgment.
- Its design system generator (`--design-system` flag) would conflict with your existing `design-system.md` ÔÇö don't run it without first showing me the output.
- Color/font outputs from the skill are always treated as proposals, never applied silently.

---

**Practical next move:** Say the word on Phase 2a (`layout.tsx`) and the skill will automatically inform the accessibility and interaction layer as I build it, Superman.

---

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
auto_search.py ÔÇö HarmonyForge ui-ux-pro-max auto-dispatcher
Runs as a UserPromptSubmit hook. Reads the user prompt from stdin (Claude Code
hook JSON payload), maps it to the most relevant skill domain, and surfaces
top-3 results as labelled suggestions.

Output is injected into Claude's context automatically via the hook system.
Fails silently on any error ÔÇö never blocks the user's prompt.
"""

import sys
import json
import subprocess
import os
import re

SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
SEARCH_SCRIPT = os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, "search.py")
PYTHON = sys.executable

# Keyword Ôćĺ domain priority map (higher weight = stronger signal)
DOMAIN_MAP = {
    "ux": [
        "accessibility", "keyboard", "focus", "aria", "screen reader",
        "touch", "hover", "tooltip", "animation", "interaction", "tab",
        "focus ring", "drag", "drop", "gesture", "pour",
    ],
    "react": [
        "component", "hook", "useeffect", "usestate", "ref", "memo",
        "render", "performance", "zustand", "resize", "observer",
        "vexflow", "canvas", "effect", "store",
    ],
    "web": [
        "layout", "responsive", "scroll", "grid", "flex", "dom",
        "svg", "split pane", "sidebar", "dock", "toolbar", "panel",
        "breakpoint", "overflow", "position", "fixed", "sticky",
    ],
    "style": [
        "style", "theme", "dark mode", "light mode", "nocturne", "sonata",
        "color", "palette", "gradient", "shadow", "glassmorphism",
        "brutalism", "minimalism", "visual", "aesthetic",
    ],
}

# Domains that carry token risk ÔÇö output gets the suggestion label
LABELLED_DOMAINS = {"style", "color", "typography"}


def extract_prompt(stdin_bytes: bytes) -> str:
    """Try to parse Claude Code hook JSON payload; fall back to raw text."""
    text = stdin_bytes.decode("utf-8", errors="replace").strip()
    if not text:
        return ""
    try:
        payload = json.loads(text)
        # Claude Code hook payload shapes:
        return (
            payload.get("prompt")
            or payload.get("message")
            or payload.get("content")
            or ""
        )
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        return text  # raw prompt fallback


def best_domain(prompt: str) -> tuple[str, int]:
    lower = prompt.lower()
    scores: dict[str, int] = {}
    for domain, keywords in DOMAIN_MAP.items():
        scores[domain] = sum(1 for kw in keywords if kw in lower)
    best = max(scores, key=lambda d: scores[d])
    return best, scores[best]


def run_search(query: str, domain: str, n: int = 3) -> str:
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            [PYTHON, SEARCH_SCRIPT, query, "--domain", domain, "-n", str(n)],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=10,
            cwd=os.path.dirname(SCRIPT_DIR),  # project root
        )
        return result.stdout.strip()
    except Exception:
        return ""


def main() -> None:
    raw = sys.stdin.buffer.read()
    prompt = extract_prompt(raw)

    if not prompt:
        return

    # Truncate query to first 80 chars for cleaner search signal
    query = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", prompt[:80]).strip()
    domain, score = best_domain(prompt)

    # Skip if no meaningful keyword signal ÔÇö avoid noise on non-design prompts
    if score == 0:
        domain = "ux"  # always safe fallback

    output = run_search(query, domain)
    if not output:
        return

    label = "[ui-ux-pro-max suggestion]" if domain in LABELLED_DOMAINS else "[ui-ux-pro-max]"
    header = f"\n{label} Auto-scan Ôćĺ domain: {domain} | query: \"{query[:50]}\"\n"
    # Limit total output to keep context clean
    print(header + output[:1200])


if __name__ == "__main__":
    try:
        main()
    except Exception:
        pass  # always silent failure

```

# HarmonyForge ÔÇö Persistent Memory

## Behavioral Commitments (Option A)
- **Always run ui-ux-pro-max search.py proactively** before writing any UI component.
  Map the task to the right domain first (`ux`, `react`, `web`, `style`), run top-3
  results, surface them before generating code. Never skip this step for Phase 2+.
- **Style/color results** Ôćĺ always prefix `[ui-ux-pro-max suggestion]`, never auto-apply.
- **UX/react/web results** Ôćĺ safe to adopt directly, no token risk.

## Hook Setup (Option B)
- `settings.json` has a second `UserPromptSubmit` hook that auto-runs `auto_search.py`
  when design keywords are detected in the prompt.
- Script: `.claude/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/auto_search.py`
- Keyword matcher covers: build, design, implement, layout, component, button, modal,
  sidebar, card, nav, dock, tooltip, theme, style, create, scaffold, render, responsive,
  hover, drag, focus, accessibility.

## Project State
- **Phase 1**: ~90% complete. Tokens, fonts, shadows all in globals.css. No React
  components exist yet. Logo redesign is a design asset (not a code task).
- **Phase 2**: Not started. Next: layout.tsx Ôćĺ page.tsx Ôćĺ theme toggle Ôćĺ bottom dock.
- **WCAG flags**: accent (#ffb300) on Sonata bg (#fdf5e6) = 1.66:1 ÔÇö fails non-text
  contrast (┬ž1.4.11). Needs Superman approval before any fix.

## Design System
- Locked palettes: Nocturne (dark) / Sonata (light) ÔÇö never override with ad-hoc values.
- Typography: Instrument Serif (brand), Inter (body/labels), Satoshi/SF Pro (system/data).
- Tokens live in: `src/app/globals.css` (Tailwind v4 CSS-first).
- Full spec: `design-system.md` (root) ÔÇö do not overwrite.

## Key Files
- `src/app/globals.css` ÔÇö all design tokens (Sonata/Nocturne, shadows, spacing, radius)
- `src/styles/fonts.css` ÔÇö font imports
- `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` ÔÇö code style + ui-ux-pro-max suggestion protocol
- `.claude/rules/music-theory.md` ÔÇö Theory Named strategy, SATB constraints
- `.claude/rules/hci-protocol.md` ÔÇö preview Ôćĺ gate Ôćĺ execute workflow
- `PLAN.md` ÔÇö 4-phase redesign roadmap with Claude prompts
